intent.summary: Aggressive Python-only AIDL ruleset focused on lower output token cost.
intent.training_direction: This branch intentionally optimizes the AIDL -> Python path first.
intent.temporary_note: This is an exploration branch for aggressive lowering, not the stable reversible branch.
intent.scaling_risk: Rules that do not pay for themselves in token savings should be removed.
intent.current_constraint: Keep only rules that are useful in practice for Python generation.

principles: Prefer lower token output over readability.
Keep direct Python passthrough available via a file-level py header escape hatch.
If multiple adjacent statements share the same kind, prefer grouped operators when they materially reduce token cost.
Retain only rules with clear practical value or measured savings.
This branch optimizes AIDL -> Python, not Python -> AIDL reversibility.

py_header.python: If the first physical line is exactly py, the rest of the file is treated as raw Python.

_flat_mode.python: left-aligned line + trailing digit for next-line indentation spaces
notes: Flat mode keeps every line left-aligned.
The trailing digit encodes the next line's indentation space count.
In the current Python path, the next indentation count should be even.
When the next line is top-level, the current serializer omits the trailing suffix.

F.python_shape: [x for x in seq if cond(x)]

FM.python_shape: [expr(x) for x in seq if cond(x)]

A.rewrite_hint: Use for existential checks over a sequence.

E.rewrite_hint: Use for universal checks over a sequence.

SFM.python_shape: sum(expr(x) for x in seq if cond(x))
SFM.rewrite_hint: Use instead of sum over filter-map.

CF.python_shape: sum(1 for x in seq if cond(x))
CF.rewrite_hint: Use instead of len(filter(...)) or counting comprehension results.

DFM.python_shape: {key(x): value(x) for x in seq if cond(x)}
DFM.rewrite_hint: Use for dict construction from a filtered sequence.

CO.python_shape: value if value is not None else fallback

KV.python_shape: {k_expr: v_expr for k, v in obj.items()}

KVF.python_shape: {k_expr: v_expr for k, v in obj.items() if cond}

ANYN.python_shape: any(it is None for it in (...))
ANYN.rewrite_hint: Use when any value may be None.

ALLNN.python_shape: all(it is not None for it in (...))
ALLNN.rewrite_hint: Use when all values must be non-None.

CNTNN.python_shape: sum(1 for it in (...) if it is not None)

SEQ.rewrite_hint: Use for common sequential model definitions.

preferred_rewrites.python: sum(expr(x) for x in seq if pred(x))
len([x for x in seq if pred(x)])
{k: v for k, v in obj.items()}
{k: v for k, v in obj.items() if cond}
value if value is not None else fallback
repeated is None / is not None checks

preferred_rewrites.aidl: Prefer F / M / FM / S / A / E / SFM / CF / DFM / KV / KVF when the pattern is exactly reversible

preferred_rewrites.python: elif / with / try / except / finally / class
preferred_rewrites.aidl: Prefer native block syntax instead of py escapes
Prefer SEQ / C2 / RL / FL / LN / CE / SGD / DEV / TR / TI

preferred_rewrites.python: Runs of adjacent assignments or expression-only statements
preferred_rewrites.aidl: Prefer == and >> when grouping removes repeated prefixes without obscuring the code

restrictions: Stay within the current Python translator subset.
Do not invent macros that have no clear practical payoff.
If a construct is unsupported, use the narrowest possible raw Python fallback.
Do not emit Python and AIDL side by side unless explicitly requested.
